title: brain db rebuild — --json success output
description: Rebuild the vault-projection tables from Markdown inside a single transaction, replacing only the projection set that exists at that point. Intrinsically idempotent and crash-safe: a crash mid-rebuild leaves the prior projection readable. NEVER touches the ledger tables or db_schema_migrations. Acquires the exclusive vault-maintenance lock; db migrate must have completed first. (The --from-git audit-ref fold / ledger reconstruction is a later-phase disaster-recovery capability and is deliberately NOT part of the Phase-1 contract.) With --from-git it runs in disaster-recovery mode: a best-effort projection rebuild from the committed git state that surfaces every non-derivable fact (the ledger/audit classes, unreadable files, dangling links) as an explicit gap instead of failing, so a partial history can never masquerade as complete.

fromGit.description: True when the rebuild ran in --from-git disaster-recovery mode (best-effort projection rebuild from canonical Markdown, surfacing every non-derivable fact as a gap).

gaps.description: Facts the --from-git rebuild could not reconstruct from canonical Markdown (always includes the ledger/audit classes; never silently dropped). Absent/empty for a plain rebuild.

x-atlas-contract.summary: Transactionally rebuild vault-projection tables from Markdown; never touches ledger tables.

flags.description: Disaster-recovery rebuild: reconstruct the projection from the committed git state (Markdown is SSOT) BEST-EFFORT, surfacing every fact git cannot reconstruct (the ledger/audit classes, tampered files, dangling links) as an explicit gap rather than failing hard.

sideEffects: replaces the vault-projection tables inside one transaction
emits exactly one run.projection git-ref audit event (executed projection-only command)
prohibitedEffects: never touches ledger tables or db_schema_migrations
a crash mid-rebuild must leave the prior projection readable

errorCodes.when: the vault is missing/unreadable so projections cannot be re-derived
the SQLite store file is missing or unopenable
the exclusive vault-maintenance lock is held by a live process
the rebuild transaction failed and was rolled back
